dataset: Real audio files (Beethoven Moonlight Sonata, LibriVox Art of War, or Wikimedia Commons Good Morning)

pi_items.source: verifier.py checks boom position against metadata.get('boom_target_sample', 0)
verifier.py checks boom position within metadata.get('boom_sample_tolerance', 10)
verifier.py checks lavalier alignment against metadata.get('lav_target_sample', 220)
verifier.py checks lavalier alignment within metadata.get('lav_sample_tolerance', 20)
verifier.py checks boom gain against metadata.get('boom_target_gain_db', -6.0)
verifier.py checks lav gain against metadata.get('lav_target_gain_db', 0.0)
verifier.py uses metadata.get('gain_tolerance_db', 1.5) as the tolerance for both track gains

Task instructions ask to name the group Dialogue Sync, matching the keywords. However, verifier.py is_edit_grouped() function only checks that a RouteGroup exists with edit=1, ignoring the group name.
verifier.py uses metadata.get('pass_threshold', 70) to determine final pass/fail status

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real audio files downloaded from public domains (LibriVox, Internet Archive, or Wikimedia Commons). Through analysis of verifier.py, it was confirmed that the expected alignment requires the boom mic at sample 0 (±10) and the lavalier mic exactly at sample 220 (±20). Gain staging requirements (-6dB for boom, 0dB for lav with ±1.5dB tolerance) were correctly enforced by the verifier logic. While the task instructs the agent to name the track group Dialogue Sync, the verifier only checks that the tracks share an edit-enabled group regardless of its name.
